app.title: OptimeFlow(s) Coherent Dynamics
app.description: OptimeFlow(s) Coherent Dynamics · CU 16×16 Sᵢ PWA simulation with structural pulse, levels, export, and offline use.

numbers.downloadCSVTitle: Download CSV of the current table
numbers.exportRSnapshotTitle: Export r_used/r_si for the current instant
numbers.exportRLogTitle: Export the history if 'Log r' is active

explain.manualTitle:
Manual – “Controlled seeding + Level {{lvl}}”
• Configure 16 seeds (G1..G16).
• Evolution follows the logic of Level {{lvl}}.

levels.0:
Level 0 – “Minimal single seed”
• Only G1·S1 = 0.000000000000010 is injected (auto‑reseed when selecting Level 0).
• Only group 1 evolves through internal memory (local progressive sum).
• Useful for observing anchors/oscillations with minimal excitation.
levels.1:
Level 1 – “S₁₆ as previous sink”
• Global suma_s16_anterior (previous step).
• r_usado = inv(suma_s16_anterior + acumulado_random_actual + r_iniciales_previos[g], “sin”).
• S₁ = sin(r_usado) (or r_usado if sin→off). S₂..S₁₅: inv(r_usado, formula) → sin(r).
• S₁₆ = attenuated formula (or r_usado if sin→off).
levels.2:
Level 2 – “Uniform global sum”
• suma_si_anterior[s] and global sum.
• S₂..S₁₅: inv(r_usado + suma_total_global, formula).
levels.3:
Level 3 – “Progressive sum by position”
• suma_si_anterior[s] by index.
• S₂..S₁₅: inv(r_usado + Σk≤s suma_si_anterior[k], formula).
levels.4:
Level 4 – “Progressive + memory from 3rd cycle”
• Like level 3, adding si_valores_previos[g][s] from the 3rd cycle onward.
levels.5:
Level 5 – “Continuous feedback”
• Progressive by s and always adds si_valores_previos[g][s].
levels.6:
Level 6 – “Local loop S₁₆→S₁”
• r_usado depends on the same group’s S₁₆ (previous step).
• S₂..S₁₅ with local internal progressive sum.
levels.7:
Level 7 – “Lateral coupling (ring)”
• Each group receives from the left neighbor by index.
levels.8:
Level 8 – “Internal mirror (cross)”
• S₂..S₁₅ use their symmetric pair sym=15−s from the previous step.

manual.hint: Tip: use small values (e.g., 0.0001 … 0.01). Allowed range: ±0.99999999999999.

hud.keys: Keys: A=Reset, Space=Play/Pause, E=Export, R=Slow down, S=Step

coherence.title: Universal Coherence
coherence.text: May this work serve as gratitude and commitment: to think, feel, and act in the same direction, without "ornaments"; to keep creating with ethics, joy, and respect for everything that connects us.

privacy.title: Privacy
privacy.body:
OptimeFlow(s) · Coherent Dynamics runs 100% locally (in your browser). We do not send the values you enter to external servers, nor do we use analytics or trackers.

Data stored on this device

  • Preferences: visual theme and language.
  • Notes: stored in localStorage if you choose to save them.
  • Manual seeds: stored in localStorage for reuse.
  • Offline cache (if you install it as a PWA): the browser may store app files so it can open without connection.

Browser permissions

  • Clipboard: only used when you press “Copy”.
  • Storage: to save preferences, notes, and seeds in localStorage.

How to delete your data

You can delete everything from the browser settings (site data: storage/localStorage and cache) or by uninstalling the PWA, if you installed it.

language.title: Language
language.description: Choose the interface language. If a language is not available yet, Spanish will be used.

themes.title: Themes
themes.description: Choose a visual theme. It applies to the whole app and will be remembered on this device.

si16.title: The 16 S(i)
si16.body:
The 16 S(i) are the dynamic model through which the Universal Coherence Project seeks to describe heterogeneous phenomena without fragmenting them into disconnected pieces. They do not appear as isolated “stages” or as a closed doctrine, but rather as a minimal functional grammar for reading how a system is driven, sustained, reorganized, crosses thresholds, enters resonance, and reaches more complex degrees of integration.

The essay always distinguishes two complementary planes. On the one hand, the functional plane: each S(i) fulfills a universal role within the system’s dynamics. On the other, the representational plane: each function can be visualized through mathematical families that help simulate and make the dynamics visible, without ever confusing the representation with the phenomenon itself.

How they are ordered

The model is organized in blocks. S₁–S₄ form the vibrational base: minimal impulse, aggregation, integrity, and dynamic balance. S₅–S₈ introduce memory, scaling, dispersion, and threshold. S₉–S₁₂ gather the operational core and the adaptive-resonant zone: dynamic accumulation, plasticity, tuning, and interconnection. S₁₃–S₁₆ express complex organization, flow management, structural support, and maximum integrated projection.

Put simply: the 16 S(i) do not seek to “attach pretty labels”, but to offer a structural reading of what a system is doing at each moment and how its layers relate to one another.

The key of the structural pulse

In the essay’s operational reading, S₉ occupies the center: it is dynamic accumulation, effective history, trigger core, and resynchronization point. That is why the real dynamics are not understood only as a line S₁ → … → S₁₆. The text proposes two complementary readings:

  • Sequential reading: useful for exposition, ordering, and pedagogical description of the model’s full deployment.
  • Structural pulse: S₉ directly triggers S₁ and S₁₆; afterward, the system returns to S₉ through two simultaneous paths: S₁ → S₂ → … → S₈ → S₉ and S₁₆ → S₁₅ → … → S₁₀ → S₉.

Thus, the extremes are not “origin and end” in a narrative sense, but functional extremes. And the intermediate functions are not the path of the initial trigger, but the reintegration channels through which the system returns to the core, readjusts itself, and sustains coherence.

What they make it possible to read

The model serves to map physical, biological, social, cognitive, or symbolic phenomena without requiring them all to speak the same disciplinary language. What is compared is not a “magical” essence, but an architecture of relationships: support from below, regulation from above, recursivity, thresholds, resonances, redistribution, capacity for reorganization, and criterion of fruit.

In that sense, the 16 S(i) work as an operational module of the framework: they help move from structural intuition to a more orderly, visualizable, and testable exploration.

Relation to this PWA

This app takes the model as a visual and computational container. What you see on screen is a heuristic implementation for exploring behavior, comparing paths, and making the dynamics visible. The visualization does not exhaust the essay: it translates it into an interactive environment where you can observe seeds, levels, return, pulse, resonances, intermediate values, and reading modes.

Framework essays

The 16 Simultaneous Structural Functions (S(i)) DOI: 10.5281/zenodo.18751249 Open record on Zenodo Open DOI
Ontological and epistemological foundations of Universal Coherence (CU) DOI: 10.5281/zenodo.18702121 Open record on Zenodo Open DOI
The Universal Coherence Scale of Limited Precision DOI: 10.5281/zenodo.18714577 Open record on Zenodo Open DOI

whatSeeing.title: What am I seeing?
whatSeeing.body:
What you are seeing is an interactive simulation of 16 groups × 16 functions. Each column G1…G16 represents a group or trajectory; each row S1…S16 represents a structural function within that group. The app recalculates the system step by step and shows you two things at once: numerical values and visual form.

1. Top number band

The large table at the top is the live state of the system. Each cell shows the current value of a function Sᵢ within a group Gⱼ. If you enable Show r, each cell adds below the associated r used internally for that function at that instant.

From that same band you can copy the table, download it as CSV, export r snapshots, record history, and export a combined log of values + r.

2. Control Panel

The floating panel governs the behavior of the simulation. Start, Pause, Step, Slow down, and Reset control the rhythm. Level changes the coupling logic between groups and memories. Step FPS sets how many steps per second the app tries to execute. Decimals adjusts visible precision.

The Structural pulse check changes the flow reading: when disabled it keeps the ascending reading S₁ → … → S₁₆; when enabled it uses the pulse described in the essay, starting at S₉, directly triggering S₁ and S₁₆, and converging back to the core.

3. Explanation panel

On the left there is a contextual explanation of the active level. That panel summarizes what kind of memory, coupling, or return the simulation is using in that mode. If you choose Manual, the seeds block also opens so you can define the initial values of G1…G16 yourself.

4. Central canvas / viewer

The viewer draws each group as a family of concentric ellipses. In All (16) view, each group occupies a cell of the 4×4 grid. In Group view, only one is isolated and a side table appears with that group’s exact data.

The size of each stroke is calculated from the internal magnitude you have chosen for drawing. If Draw formula (Sᵢ) is disabled, the canvas uses the magnitude of r. If it is enabled, it uses the computed formula value for each function. Colors help differentiate levels and groups; geometry helps perceive how layers expand, dampen, or reorganize.

5. Viewer HUD

In the upper corner of the viewer you see a HUD with quick reading: STEP, SECONDS, average S1, average S16, number of RESONANCES, active flow, last key, and slowdown state. In this implementation, the resonance counter increases when a group simultaneously falls within the internal ranges defined for S16 and S1.

6. Reading tools

  • Show r: shows the r used by each function.
  • Log r: stores an exportable history.
  • sin → off: shows a “bare” r instead of passing it through the usual trigonometric output.
  • Draw formula (Sᵢ): changes the canvas drawing basis.
  • Manual: lets you seed each group and then run the logic of the level you choose.
  • Notes: gives you a local notebook to document observations, hypotheses, or readings.

7. How to read the screen without confusing planes

The PWA does not aim to be “the thing” itself, but an interactive container for exploring patterns. The numeric layer gives you traceability; the visual layer gives you perception of form; the level changes the interaction logic; and the structural pulse lets you compare how a sequence is lived and how the return to the core functions.

In short: at the top you see quantified state, in the center you see dynamic form, on the left you see contextual interpretation, and on the right you keep reading memory. Everything runs locally, step by step, so you can observe the system’s coherence as a process and not only as a final result.

mobileWarning.title: Mobile warning
mobileWarning.body:
OptimeFlow(s) Coherent Dynamics is designed for Desktop. The canvas visualization, the full number band, the floating panel, and fine interaction are not yet adapted for mobile.

On phone or tablet you can open it and consult content, but to really work with it, compare it, and see it as it was conceived, using a computer is recommended.

toasts.langFallback: Translation not available yet; using Spanish.

flow.hint: S₉ triggers S₁ and S₁₆, and the return converges again at S₉.
flow.noteLinear:
Sequential reading active
The simulation keeps the ascending path S₁ → … → S₁₆ as an experiential and pedagogical reading.
flow.noteStructural:
Structural pulse active
The simulation starts at S₉, directly triggers S₁ and S₁₆, and returns to the core through S₂…S₈ and S₁₅…S₁₀.
